tool.name: Kraken Binarize

param.label: Threshold
param.help: Cutoff used to classify pixels as black or white after background normalization. Increase this value to classify more pixels as black, which can recover faint text but may add noise; decrease it to keep more pixels white, which can reduce noise but may remove faint strokes.

param.label: Zoom (scale factor) for background estimation
param.help: Lower values downsample the page more strongly, producing a smoother and faster background estimate; higher values use more image detail.

param.label: Estimated character / text scale
param.help: Increase this value for larger text or broader foreground features; decrease it for smaller text or finer foreground details.

param.label: Border fraction to ignore
param.help: Increase this value to exclude more edge artifacts such as scanner borders, shadows, or dark margins; decrease it to include more of the page border.

param.label: Percentile for filters
param.help: Higher percentile biases the estimate more toward lighter background regions; decrease it to make the estimate less biased toward bright pixels.

param.label: Range for filters
param.help: Increase it for a smoother, broader background estimate. Decrease it for stronger local adaptation; results may then be affected more by small variations, noise, or paper texture.

param.label: Percentile for black estimation
param.help: Increase it to use a brighter foreground estimate, which may reduce the influence of very dark noise or artifacts; decrease it to use a darker foreground estimate, which may better preserve dark text strokes but can be more affected by dark speckles or stains.

param.label: Percentile for white estimation
param.help: Increase it to use a brighter background estimate, which may help with very light page backgrounds but can be affected by bright artifacts or glare; decrease it to use a darker background estimate, which may be more robust to bright artifacts but can reduce contrast for faint text.

has_text.text: multi-page TIF(F) input is not supported

tool.help:
**What it does**
kraken is an open-source, turn-key automatic text recognition (ATR) system optimized for historical and non-Latin script writing. This tool only exposes Kraken's binarization command.
Binarization converts the input image/document into a binary image suitable for downstream segmentation and OCR steps.
For PDF inputs, this tool returns a Galaxy list collection of PNG images, one for each page.
**License**
Kraken is licensed under the Apache-2.0 license.
**Algorithmic background**
kraken's ``binarize`` command is derived from OCRopus/ocropy's ``nlbin``, a preprocessing script designed for degraded and historical book pages.
